ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301776 represents beer as a general category; no specific entry for Wusu brand lager exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0KL covers beer as a category. No brand-specific Wusu code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 53410008 represents beer as a substance; no brand-specific code for Wusu exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml bottle of Wusu Beer, equivalent to approximately 330 g, as typically served at Lanzhou beef noodle restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beer, regular, all (USDA SR Legacy, NDB No. 14003)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Wusu Beer is a Chinese lager from Xinjiang not individually listed in any approved primary regional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). USDA SR Legacy entry for regular beer (lager) is used as the closest approved secondary source. Wusu Beer is a standard lager with approximately 3.6% alcohol by volume in its standard variant, consistent with USDA lager values. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14003 (beer, regular, all).

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Beer (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 66 (medium) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2002 International GI table (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008) for regular beer. Wusu Beer is a standard lager; no brand-specific GI measurement exists. The GI of regular beer (66) is used as the analogous estimate. GL per serving calculated as: (66 × 3.6 g carbohydrate per 100g × 330g serving) / 100 = 7.84, rounded to 7.9.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit alcohol consumption; regular beer such as Wusu contains ethanol which can acutely raise blood pressure, and clinical guidelines recommend no more than one standard drink per day for women and two for men.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume beer with caution as alcohol can cause hypoglycaemia when taken without food, and the moderate glycemic index (66) of regular beer contributes to postprandial glucose variability; consumption should be limited and always accompanied by a carbohydrate-containing meal.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should restrict alcohol intake; while low-to-moderate beer consumption has been associated with modest HDL elevation in some studies, excess alcohol increases triglycerides and blood pressure, and current cardiovascular guidelines recommend minimising or avoiding alcohol.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wusu Beer (乌苏啤酒, Wūsū Píjiǔ) is a Chinese lager beer produced by the Xinjiang Wusu Beer Company, originating from Ürümqi, Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region, China. It is one of the most popular regional beers in China and has gained a cult following for its strong, full-bodied flavour relative to standard Chinese lagers. Wusu Beer is brewed from water, malted barley, hops, yeast, and rice, following a standard lager fermentation process. It is carbonated and typically served chilled. The beer has a crisp, slightly bitter taste with a mild malt sweetness and a clean finish. In Singapore, Wusu Beer is commonly served at Lanzhou beef noodle restaurants as a beverage pairing with the rich, spiced beef broth noodle dishes. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 43 kcal, 0.5 g protein, 0 g fat, and 3.6 g carbohydrate, with a low sodium content of 4 mg. The glycemic index is approximately 66 (medium category), though the glycemic load per standard 330 ml serving is approximately 7.9. Clinically, Wusu Beer is an alcoholic beverage and is not suitable for individuals who abstain from alcohol for medical, religious, or personal reasons. Individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease should exercise caution and limit consumption in line with clinical guidelines. It is not halal and contains gluten from barley malt.
common_user_queries: Is Wusu Beer available in Singapore?
What are the calories in Wusu Beer?
What does Wusu Beer taste like?
meal_context: beverage pairing with Lanzhou beef noodles

confidence_reason: Wusu Beer is a commercially produced Chinese lager not individually listed in any approved primary regional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for regular beer (lager), which is a valid approved secondary source. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular beer as an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium because the match is adapted rather than exact.
notes: fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for regular beer; beer contains no fat by composition (naturally zero). saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all 0.0: naturally zero as beer contains no fat fraction. sugar_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer; residual sugars are fermented out during brewing (confirmed zero). dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer (naturally zero, no fibre in beer). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 and vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 and vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as beer is a plant-derived fermented beverage. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples' to 'Beverages' as Wusu Beer is an alcoholic beverage. The input cuisine_tags included 'Drinks & Beverages' supporting this correction. Served at Lan Ting Xu Lanzhou Beef Noodles, Lazada One, Singapore (field survey data). Price SGD 13.80 per bottle as listed on field survey data. Wusu Beer is not halal; it contains alcohol and is not suitable for Muslim consumers. Gluten is present from barley malt. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no brand-specific codes exist for Wusu Beer in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT.
